data.title: Milan Station Shifts Hong Kong Branch Share Registrar to Tricor
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Milan Station Holdings Limited ( (HK:1150) ).

Milan Station Holdings Limited has announced a change to its Hong Kong branch share registrar, part of the core infrastructure that supports the administration and transfer of its listed shares. The move signals an operational shift in how the company will manage share registrations and related shareholder services in Hong Kong.

With effect from 4 May 2026, Tricor Investor Services Limited will assume the role of Hong Kong branch share registrar, becoming the new point of contact for share transfer registrations and the collection of uncollected share certificates. Shareholders must direct transfer applications and collect any outstanding certificates from Tricor from that date, marking an important administrative transition for investors in the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1150) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.16 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Milan Station Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1150 Stock Forecast page.

More about Milan Station Holdings Limited

Milan Station Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, with its shares traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company is governed by a board comprising executive directors and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical listed-company corporate governance structure.

data.title: KKCG Maritime Reports Low Initial Uptake in Partial Tender Offer for Ferretti Shares
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ) is now available.

KKCG Maritime has launched a voluntary conditional partial tender offer to acquire up to 52,132,861 shares of Ferretti S.p.A., equal to 15.4% of the yacht maker’s share capital, and by 1 April 2026 had received valid acceptances for 354,036 shares, representing only about 0.10% of shares outstanding and 0.68% of the maximum offer size. The offeror, which already directly holds 14.5% of Ferretti, reported no additional market purchases or securities lending beyond the tendered shares, and signalled that further updates on acceptances, provisional results and final allocation ratios will follow, leaving the overall outcome and potential impact on Ferretti’s shareholder base still uncertain.

The relatively low initial take-up may indicate cautious sentiment among existing shareholders regarding the offer terms or Ferretti’s valuation, and the final acceptance levels will determine whether KKCG Maritime can materially increase its strategic stake. For minority investors and market watchers, the evolving response to the partial tender will shed light on control dynamics and confidence in Ferretti’s long-term prospects within the competitive luxury yacht industry.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is a yacht manufacturer listed in Hong Kong, operating in the luxury maritime sector with a focus on high-end recreational vessels. Its shares trade under stock code 09638.HK and it attracts strategic investors interested in the premium boating and yachting market.

Learn more about YACHT stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: BrainAurora joins Beijing Frontier BCI Institute to accelerate clinical translation of brain-computer interface technologies
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BrainAurora Medical Technology Limited ( (HK:6681) ) just unveiled an announcement.

BrainAurora Medical Technology Limited has become a strategic partner of the newly established Beijing Frontier Brain-Computer Interface Institute, founded under the initiative of Xuanwu Hospital, Capital Medical University, to drive breakthroughs and clinical translation in brain-computer interface technologies. The partnership aligns BrainAurora’s cognitive impairment digital therapeutics expertise with Beijing’s push to build a leading, world-class BCI research and development hub.

Under a strategic cooperation agreement signed on March 27, 2026, BrainAurora and the Institute will jointly develop key initiatives such as a multimodal neurological disease database, a neuroelectrophysiological large-scale model, and closed-loop neuromodulation BCI products. The collaboration is expected to accelerate full-chain clinical translation of BCI technologies in China, address the needs of more than 100 million patients with neurological disorders, and strengthen an innovation ecosystem that tightly integrates engineering, medical sciences, and industry-academia cooperation.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6681) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BrainAurora Medical Technology Limited stock, see the HK:6681 Stock Forecast page.

More about BrainAurora Medical Technology Limited

BrainAurora Medical Technology Limited is a China-focused medical technology company specializing in digital therapeutics for cognitive impairment, combining brain science with artificial intelligence. Through its subsidiaries, including Zhejiang BrainAurora Medical Technology Co., Ltd., the group targets the rapidly developing brain-computer interface sector and broader high-end medical equipment market, with an emphasis on clinical applications in medical rehabilitation.

data.title: Mexan’s Controlling Shareholder Moves to Place Entire Stake in Major Ownership Shake-Up
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Mexan ( (HK:0022) ).

Mexan Limited’s controlling shareholder, Winland Wealth (BVI) Limited, which is indirectly wholly owned by chairman and executive director Lun Yiu Kay Edwin, has entered into a placing agreement with Advent Securities (Hong Kong) Limited. The agreement covers the potential placing of between about 928.7 million and 1.33 billion existing shares at HK$0.17 each to independent investors.

The planned transaction represents up to approximately 67.47% of Mexan’s issued share capital and all of the shares currently held by the controlling shareholder, meaning that, if fully executed, Winland Wealth will cease to hold any shares and will no longer be a controlling shareholder. The placing agent has committed to ensure that no individual placee, alone or with concert parties, will hold 30% or more of Mexan’s issued share capital after completion, signaling an intention to diversify the shareholding base and avoid triggering mandatory takeover obligations.

Completion of the placing is scheduled on or before 14 April 2026, subject to the terms of the agreement, and the company has cautioned that the placing may or may not proceed. The potential exit of the controlling shareholder, and consequent shift to a more dispersed ownership structure, could materially reshape Mexan’s governance dynamics and market positioning, with investors advised to exercise caution when dealing in the shares during this transition period.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0022) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.28 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mexan stock, see the HK:0022 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mexan

Mexan Limited, listed in Hong Kong under stock code 22, is a Hong Kong-based company whose shares are held by a controlling shareholder through Winland Wealth (BVI) Limited. The board is led by chairman and executive director Lun Yiu Kay Edwin, with additional executive and independent non-executive directors overseeing corporate governance and compliance with local listing regulations.

The company operates under the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Securities and Futures Ordinance, indicating its focus on maintaining listing status and adherence to market disclosure rules. Its shareholder base and control structure are central to its corporate profile, with significant ownership previously concentrated in the hands of the controlling shareholder via the BVI entity.

Mexan’s governance structure features a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard corporate practices in Hong Kong’s listed market. This framework positions the company within the broader Hong Kong capital market, where changes in controlling ownership can materially affect investor perception and trading dynamics.

Average Trading Volume: 7,655,369

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$487.7M

JD.com, Inc. has priced an offering of CNY10 billion in CNY-denominated senior unsecured notes to be sold to certain non-U.S. investors in offshore transactions under Regulation S. data.title: Melco International Strengthens Governance With Formal Audit Committee Mandate
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Melco International ( (HK:0200) ).

Melco International has formalised detailed terms of reference for its Audit Committee, defining its composition, responsibilities and powers in line with Hong Kong Stock Exchange governance requirements. The committee must comprise at least three non‑executive directors, mostly independent, with at least one member holding professional accounting or financial management expertise, and its chair and membership are appointed and removed by the board.

The Audit Committee is empowered to investigate company activities, access all books and records, obtain independent professional advice and report suspected fraud, internal control failures or legal breaches to the board. Its duties include overseeing the appointment and independence of external auditors, setting policies on non‑audit services, meeting auditors without executives present, and monitoring the integrity of the company’s financial reporting, reinforcing oversight and investor confidence in Melco’s governance framework.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Meitu ( (HK:1357) ) has provided an announcement.

Meitu has granted 16,456,530 share awards, equivalent to about 0.36% of its issued share capital excluding treasury shares, to employees and core service providers under its share award scheme, with vesting starting in April 2027. The awards, deliverable through a mix of new shares and treasury shares at the Board’s discretion, are intended to reward contributors to 2025 adjusted net profit growth, align interests with shareholders, conserve cash while expanding overseas via external consultants, and remain within Hong Kong listing rule limits, leaving 392,509,341 shares available for future grants.

The grant includes a Special Grant of 1,357 share awards each to most employees and service providers, with vesting over up to 24 months and no performance targets attached. By using equity incentives rather than cash to attract and retain talent, particularly in overseas markets where it hires through service providers, Meitu aims to support its international expansion and long-term development while maintaining compliance with individual and service provider caps under the share award scheme mandate.

Meitu, Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed technology company focused on image processing, photo and video editing, and related digital services, operating through a group structure with subsidiaries and consolidated affiliates. data.description:

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited ( (HK:2788) ) has issued an update.

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for April 23, 2026, in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK$0.77 per share. Investors will also decide on the re-election of eight retiring directors, the authorisation of the board to set directors’ pay, the re-appointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and a general mandate empowering the board to issue additional shares, which could provide future funding flexibility and affect capital structure.

The proposed dividend signals management’s confidence in the company’s 2025 performance and offers a tangible return to shareholders ahead of the new financial year. The comprehensive slate of director re-elections and the renewal of the share issuance mandate underscore continuity in governance and strategic optionality, developments that are likely to be closely watched by investors assessing the company’s future growth plans and governance stability.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2788) stock is a Buy with a HK$32.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2788 Stock Forecast page.

More about Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 02788. The group operates through various subsidiaries and is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical diversified corporate structure in Hong Kong’s capital market.

An announcement from Hygieia Group Limited ( (HK:1650) ) is now available.

Hygieia Group Limited issued a clarification regarding its previously published annual results announcement for the year ended 31 December 2025, correcting an omission about the currency used for its proposed final dividend. The board confirmed that the final dividend of 0.001 per share for 2025, compared with 0.002 per share a year earlier, is denominated in Singapore dollars, and emphasized that all other information in the original results announcement remains unchanged.

The correction removes potential ambiguity for shareholders and investors about the payout currency, which is important for assessing the actual value of the dividend and comparing it with prior years. By promptly addressing the oversight and reaffirming that no other figures or disclosures have been altered, the company aims to maintain transparency and reinforce confidence in its financial reporting among stakeholders.

data.title: Greentech Technology Deepens Probe After Auditor Complaint on Past Fund Flows
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) just unveiled an update.

Greentech Technology International has issued a further update on its prolonged compliance challenges, which have led to delays in publishing its 2024 interim and annual results and its 2025 interim results, as well as a continuing suspension of trading in its shares. The company has been working under resumption guidance and has already conducted an independent forensic investigation and internal control review as part of efforts to address regulatory concerns.

In the latest development, Greentech disclosed details of an anonymous complaint lodged with its auditor, prompting the appointment of OCF as an independent investigator and now the engagement of an additional independent investigator. The allegations center on allegedly questionable fund flows at a former subsidiary between 2020 and 2021 and a purported payment to a former director from proceeds of a loan repayment, deepening scrutiny of the group’s past transactions and governance and potentially impacting investor confidence and the timetable for trading resumption.

More about Greentech Technology International Limited

Greentech Technology International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure that has included various subsidiaries. The company is currently subject to trading suspension on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong due to delayed financial reporting and ongoing regulatory and investigative matters affecting its recent interim and annual results.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$382.5M
